categoryDescription: Checks related to user authentication, MFA, passwords, and session controls

checks.name: 2SV Enforcement
checks.description: Two-step verification (2SV/MFA) should be enforced for all users to prevent account takeover via stolen credentials
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Security > Authentication > 2-step verification > Set Enforcement to 'On'
checks.referenceTitle: Google: Protect your business with 2-Step Verification

checks.name: 2SV Enrollment Rate
checks.description: All active users should have 2SV enrolled. Low enrollment rates leave accounts vulnerable to credential-based attacks
checks.recommendedValue: 95% or higher enrollment among active users
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Reporting > User Reports > Security > Review users without 2SV. Set enrollment deadline via Security > 2-Step Verification

checks.name: 2SV Method Strength
checks.description: Security keys should be the primary 2SV method. SMS and voice-based 2SV are vulnerable to SIM-swapping and interception attacks
checks.recommendedValue: Security keys enforced as primary method
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Security > Authentication > 2-step verification > Set allowed methods to 'Security key only'
checks.referenceTitle: Why SMS-based MFA is vulnerable (SIM-swap & interception)

checks.name: Password Minimum Length
checks.description: Password minimum length should be at least 12 characters to resist brute-force and dictionary attacks
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Security > Authentication > Password management > Set minimum length to 12 or higher
checks.referenceTitle: NIST password guidelines: why length resists brute force

checks.name: Password Reuse Restriction
checks.description: Users should not be able to reuse recent passwords, preventing credential cycling attacks
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Security > Authentication > Password management > Enable 'Enforce password policy at next sign-in' and restrict reuse
checks.referenceTitle: Google: Enforce and monitor password requirements

checks.name: Session Duration
checks.description: Web session duration should be limited to reduce the window for session hijacking and unauthorized access from shared devices
checks.recommendedValue: Session duration of 12 hours or less
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Security > Google Session Control > Set web session duration
checks.referenceTitle: Google: Set session length for Google services

checks.name: SSO Configuration
checks.description: If SSO is configured, it should use secure protocols and trusted identity providers
checks.recommendedValue: SAML SSO properly configured with trusted IdP
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Security > Authentication > SSO with third-party IdP > Verify configuration
checks.referenceTitle: Google: Best practices for third-party IdP configuration

checks.name: Less Secure Apps Access
checks.description: Less secure apps (apps that don't support modern authentication) should be blocked to prevent credential exposure
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Security > Authentication > Less secure apps > Set to 'Disable access to less secure apps'
checks.referenceTitle: Google: Transition from less secure apps to OAuth

checks.name: App Passwords Policy
checks.description: App-specific passwords bypass 2SV and should be controlled. If allowed, they should require 2SV enrollment first
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Security > Authentication > 2-step verification > Review app password settings
checks.referenceTitle: Google: How 2-Step Verification works with legacy apps

checks.name: Recovery Options Configuration
checks.description: User self-service recovery should be configured appropriately. Super admins should not have personal recovery options to prevent social engineering
checks.recommendedValue: Super admins: no personal recovery. Regular users: recovery options allowed with admin override
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Security > Authentication > Account recovery > Disable personal recovery for super admin OU
checks.referenceTitle: Google: Allow super administrators to recover their password
checks.affectedLabel: Super admins with personal recovery options

checks.name: Login Challenge Settings
checks.description: Login challenges should be enabled to provide additional verification when suspicious login attempts are detected
checks.recommendedValue: Login challenges enabled with employee ID or other verification
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Security > Authentication > Login challenges > Enable
checks.referenceTitle: Google: Protect accounts with security challenges

checks.name: Super Admin 2SV Enrollment
checks.description: All super admin accounts must have 2SV enrolled. Super admins have unrestricted access to all settings and data
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Reporting > User Reports > Security > Filter by admin status > Ensure all super admins have 2SV enrolled

checks.name: Stale Super Admin Accounts
checks.description: Super admin accounts that have not logged in recently may be orphaned and at risk of compromise. All super admin accounts should be actively managed
checks.recommendedValue: No super admin accounts inactive for more than 90 days
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Directory > Users > Filter by admin role > Review and remove or suspend inactive super admin accounts
checks.referenceTitle: Google Cloud: Super administrator account best practices

checks.name: 2SV Enrollment Allowed
checks.description: Users must be allowed to enroll in two-step verification. Disabling 2SV enrollment blocks MFA adoption and leaves accounts protected by passwords alone
checks.recommendedValue: 2SV enrollment allowed in all organizational units
checks.remediationSteps: Security > Authentication > 2-step verification > Set 'Allow users to turn on 2-Step Verification' to On for all organizational units

checks.name: 2SV Enrollment Grace Period
checks.description: The grace period before newly added users must enroll in 2SV should be short. A long grace period leaves accounts unprotected by MFA for an extended window after creation
checks.recommendedValue: Grace period of 7 days (168 hours) or less
checks.remediationSteps: Security > Authentication > 2-step verification > Set the new-user enrollment grace period to 7 days or less
checks.referenceTitle: Google: Set a 2-Step Verification enrollment period

checks.name: Advanced Protection Self-Enrollment
checks.description: Allowing self-enrollment in the Advanced Protection Program lets high-risk users adopt the strongest available account protections without admin intervention
checks.recommendedValue: Advanced Protection self-enrollment allowed in all organizational units
checks.remediationSteps: Security > Authentication > Advanced Protection Program > Allow users to self-enroll in Advanced Protection
checks.referenceTitle: Google: Protect users with the Advanced Protection Program

checks.name: Super Admin Account Self-Recovery
checks.description: Self-service account recovery for super admins is an account-takeover path via social engineering and should be turned off. Super admins should be recovered only by another administrator
checks.recommendedValue: Super admin self-recovery disabled in all organizational units
checks.remediationSteps: Security > Authentication > Account recovery > Turn off 'Allow super admins to recover their account' for all organizational units
checks.referenceTitle: Google: Allow super administrators to recover their password
